entries.prompt: How do you currently embody virtues (Courage, Wisdom, Justice, Temperance)
entries.response:
Courage
- Frank reflection of effort made to date
- Could challenge myself more
- Seek out discomfort & adventure
- Look for opportunities to do so (say yes to everything)


Wisdom
–  practising critical thought
do it with less cynicism, don't just expose myself to both sides, hear them.
study something new, stoicism until something else
if an interesting insight is found, share.

Justice
–  Acknowledging to self and others when I know could have been nicer.
Find a way to catch myself in the moment before having to reflect later.

Temperance
–  Set absolute rules to break bad habits, start with getting up on time
start a regime to promote consistency.
let your actions speak for you


Vision of a virtuous life
Nothing stopping me from doing so
Nothing stopping me from starting now
would need to reverse years of habit that avoided/ignored/dishonoured myself. Committing to be virtuous.
IF I do it through action  –  (I do not verbalise what I am undertaking to others) then it is not "signalling" or done for social credit, it is done for my own growth, to seek happiness with nature.
Trust that the change in me will impact/improve those close to me & will ultimately impact wider company through action and demeanour & approachablity and presenting myself out their physically and mentally available to opportunities.


Temptations & focus on self
Temptation for flavour is my perception of the rush obtained from it. I can control how I crave things by recognising a craving of any sort is a perception.

entries.prompt: The opinions of others are laced with their bias and their justification for what it may have cost them.
entries.response:
I must view others opinions/actions in the context of where they sit in the opportunity or inspiration for me to test courage, justice, temperance, wisdom. Is there anything in these contexts to be learned from them is the only question.


Living in the now / Rational response - Knowing what you know what would you have done differently
Ensure everything I do or say is considered, not against my reaction in the moment but measured against the values of courage, justice, temperance & wisdom.
And if my response or intent does not correlate to these then begin with the assumption that once I disassociate with the feelings I have from the moment I will be able to not react passionately, perhaps no reaction at all or open to going with  curiosity. Strategically it is better to hide your disagreement until the appropriate planning can take place for a response or for all relevant info to be at hand.


Pursuing virtue with no external reward
Set internal benchmarks of success for each underlying principle

entries.prompt: Reflection becomes the reward when measuring each day against benchmarks
entries.response:
Keep foster belief that happiness will be result from harmony with nature.
Foster belief that contentment is something that can be mastered using our minds through practice
Question long existing belief system of sardonic skepticism
Reverse both lifelong habit of expressing any thought

entries.prompt: Note that the "do the right thing" principle has been my ethos and does fall under stoic virtues.
entries.response:
Duty to others / Giving back
Community
Housing crisis  –  Doing subdivisions (in the future perhaps)
Boss  –  Remain flexible, can do, build morale
Team  –  Resist leaning my job, controlling emotions
Friends  –  Reaching out, catching up
Wife  –  Pulling my weight, culling what I've hoarded
Kids  –  attentino, fun, generous, curious
Parents  –  Listen, clean, appreciate, abstain
Family  –  Task list, Do - not say, Sell items, dispose of mess, commit to health /sleep/study/serenity
Nature
–  Spend any available time outside
–  Examine back yard cleanup
–  Reduce waste, review impact
–  Soak up sun & sounds each day.
Stoicism
–  Overcome bias against belief systems
–  Commit to journaling, practice, self examination.
–  Marathon not a race, Finish this book first.
–  Keep things to myself from now on.

entries.prompt: Think when you let yourself down. What would you do differently to improve your Self-respect.
entries.response:
Opinion of myself to overcome:
–  Delusional, fraud, lazy, incompetent, frail
Art pursuits  –  sketches, poetry, screenplay, filmmaking, music production, I let myself down by being too dependant on positive feedback and motivated by success. Not committing to the less fun effort to follow through and finish work, courage to face multiple rejections, to grind away at the next task even if it feels embarrassing to market or pitch your art. Not sticking it out to complete the art. Not brave enough to display for all to see and judge, and reject.
–  List out and overcome, be satisfied with working towards overcoming those beliefs improving your opinion of self.

entries.prompt: How do you concentrate on Building character
entries.response:
Quality of life  –  Peaceful, loving, caring home
Stop avoiding or putting off challenging tasks
Stop refusing opportunities on the assumption that I lack health/knowledge/means
Seek out new experiences and opportunities
Keep open to new experiences
Don't refuse things that had no perceived benefit to me
Examine things I may have passed on in the past that would have built character and plan to do them now.

entries.prompt: What a flourishing life (eudaimonia) looks like
entries.response:
Continual improvement in not necessarily large steps but actively working toward betterment
Positive Outlook
Strong Health
Capable physically
Malleable/Flexible
Things done & available for duty
Belongings lessened (Unburdened)
Position to be generous with time/money
Regimented good habits
Controlled temptation/imagination
Controlled emotion/ego/pride/expectations
Released baggage
Never missing the wonder of the moment

Steps taken toward eudaimonia today
- Reviewed List of tasks & add to them
Walk
Journal
Catch negative/cynicism and park it
Avoid couch
catch temptation/cravings and park them
Truly listen and take in surroundings
Pause for interesting or beautiful things
Always have an opinion but refrain
Share what's interesting & beautiful
Face what I'm not looking forward to with positivity and curiosity and start it.
Let emotions bounce off me, not control me
Embrace AI & Explore Capabilities
